reason: DISTRIBUTIONAL DERIVATIVE SIGN CONVENTION UNSTATED (line 382-383): The proof begins with the identity <partial_beta T^{alpha beta}, phi> = -<T^{alpha beta}, partial_beta phi>. While this IS the standard sign convention for distributional derivatives (transposing the derivative to the test function with a sign from integration by parts), the sign convention is not cited or justified. In a rigorous distributional proof, this should reference the definition of distributional derivative being used, since sign conventions vary in the literature (some authors define distributional derivatives without the sign when working with the formal adjoint). This is a minor gap: the convention used is the standard one (consistent with e.g. Schwartz, Hormander, Rudin), but stating it explicitly or referencing it would improve rigour.
